title: Monaco-2025 rule-aware legality state
summary: Inference from the 2025 FIA rules: Monaco is a special case.
rationale: Inference from the 2025 FIA rules: Monaco is a special case. In dry conditions, most races only require two slick compounds, but Monaco 2025 also requires three tyre sets; the wet/intermediate exemption still applies. A generic 'owes one stop' feature likely under-models this one-race regime shift.
implementation_hint: Add a causal state machine per driver-race with features such as `dry_rule_active`, `used_wet_or_inter`, `slick_compounds_used`, `sets_used`, `stops_completed`, `required_stops_remaining`, and interactions with `LapsRemaining_Est` and current finish-margin features. Gate the extra stop-pressure features to `Race == Monaco Grand Prix` and `Year >= 2025`.
expected_effect: Small but clean lift from better handling of Monaco rows and late-race stop-pressure calibration.
risk: Low coverage and easy overfit if Monaco naming/versioning is inconsistent; all state features must be computed only from prior laps within the race.
